auth_type.description: Authentication method for the provider connection (e.g. `api_token`, `oauth`).

collection.description: Default collection partition for synced objects. Canonical name; mirrors the deprecated `sub_tenant_id` alias.

credential_ref.description: Internal reference to the stored credential record.

database.description:
Database/Collection are the canonical v2 names for the deprecated
tenant_id/sub_tenant_id wire fields. They mirror the same values so a v2
client sees the canonical names on responses while a legacy client keeps
reading tenant_id/sub_tenant_id. Not persisted (dynamodbav:"-"): the store
builds items from tenant_id/sub_tenant_id and mirrors these on load. They
are populated at every construction point (toConnector, connectorFromItem)
rather than via MarshalJSON so Temporal's JSON data converter round-trips
Connector activity inputs without spuriously populating them.

deployment_id.description: Internal deployment context for this connector.

last_attempted_sync_at.description: RFC3339 timestamp of the most recent sync attempt (successful or not).

last_error.description: Error message from the most recent failed sync, empty string when no error.

last_successful_sync_at.description: RFC3339 timestamp of the last successful sync completion.

next_sync_at.description: RFC3339 timestamp when the next scheduled sync will run.

plan.description: Subscription plan this connector runs under.

provider_account_scope.description: Identifier for the external account (e.g. Slack workspace ID, GitHub org name). Must be distinct across connectors for the same provider.

sub_tenant_id.description: Default collection partition for synced objects. Deprecated — use `collection`.

sync_engine.description:
SyncEngine is "classic" (default, empty treated as classic) or "moveit".
See the SyncEngine* constants; the scheduler branches on it.

sync_interval_seconds.description: How frequently the scheduler triggers incremental syncs, in seconds.

tenant_id.description: Database that receives synced data. Deprecated — use `database`.

user_id.description: User that created or owns this resource.

additional_metadata.description:
AdditionalMetadata is merged into the additional_metadata (document
metadata) layer of every object synced from this resource. User-supplied
keys are shallow-merged as the base; provider-generated fields are
applied on top and always win on conflict.

backfill_chunk_interval_seconds.description:
BackfillChunkIntervalSeconds is the pacing interval persisted at configure
time so the scheduler can thread it into each chunk's workflow input.

backfill_next_chunk_at.description:
BackfillNextChunkAt is the RFC3339 time the next chunk becomes due. The
backfill workflow processes one chunk then sets this to now+interval and
exits; the connector scheduler starts the next chunk once it passes.

backfill_oldest.description:
BackfillOldest is an RFC3339 timestamp marking the oldest boundary remaining
for async historical backfill. Empty means backfill is complete or not needed.

backfill_status.description:
BackfillStatus gates the sparse ResourcesByBackfillNextChunkAt GSI: it is
set to BackfillStatusActive while a historical backfill is in progress and
removed when it completes, so only actively-backfilling resources appear in
the scheduler's due query. Pacing between chunks is driven by that scheduler
(see BackfillNextChunkAt), not by an in-workflow sleep.

collection_override.description: Routes this resource's synced objects into a specific collection, overriding the connector's. Canonical name; mirrors the deprecated `sub_tenant_id_override` alias.

database_override.description:
DatabaseOverride/CollectionOverride are the canonical v2 names for the
deprecated tenant_id_override/sub_tenant_id_override wire fields. Empty
means the resource inherits the connector's database/collection, exactly
as the deprecated fields do. Not persisted (dynamodbav:"-"): mirrored from
the tenant_id_override/sub_tenant_id_override values at construction time.

metadata.description:
Metadata is merged into the tenant metadata layer of every object synced
from this resource. User-supplied keys are shallow-merged as the base;
system defaults (connector_id, provider) are applied on top so they
always win on conflict — user keys extend the map but cannot override
system-set fields.

provider_cursor.description: Bookmark of the last synced position. Non-empty value confirms the first sync has run.

provider_metadata.description: Additional provider-supplied metadata for this resource.

resource_id.description: Resource identifier from the Discover endpoint.

resource_type.description: Type of resource within the provider (e.g. `channel`, `repo`, `linear_team`).

status.description: Current sync state of this resource (e.g. `active`, `paused`).

sub_tenant_id_override.description: Overrides the connector-level collection for objects synced from this resource.

tenant_id_override.description: Overrides the connector-level database for objects synced from this resource. Deprecated.

total.description: Total number of items across all pages.

content.description: Extracted text content of the source document.
content.example:
# Q4 Report

Revenue grew 23% quarter over quarter.

content_base64.description: Base64-encoded binary content, for binary file types.

error.description: Error message, empty string on success.

inferred_content.description: LLM-generated summary of the source content.
inferred_content.example: Summary: Q4 revenue rose 23% QoQ, driven by enterprise expansion.

presigned_url.description: Time-limited download URL for the original file.

additional_metadata_keys.description: Additional metadata keys included in the update request.

chunk_rows_matched.description: Number of MongoDB chunk rows matched by the source update.

chunk_rows_modified.description: Number of MongoDB chunk rows modified by the source update.

database_metadata_keys.description: Database metadata keys included in the update request. Canonical name; `tenant_metadata_keys` is a deprecated alias.

milvus_rows_synced.description: Number of chunk rows synced to Milvus when sync was required.

milvus_sync_required.description: Whether at least one changed database metadata field has dense or sparse embedding enabled.

milvus_synced.description: Whether the Milvus metadata sync completed. Present when sync was required.

updated.description: Whether the source metadata was updated.

entity_id.description: Unique identifier for this entity in the graph.

provider.description:
Provider is the source app the entity's evidence chunk came from (e.g.
"slack", "google", "intercom"), read from the owning Source node's
app_provider. Empty string when the evidence has no app source (plain
document / web ingest). Consumed by the dashboard to render a connector
logo inside the graph node.

is_truncated.description: Whether the response was truncated due to the result limit.

relations.description: Array of triplet groups with evidence for each relationship.

relations.context: Ada joined Acme Corp in 2024 as a staff engineer.

context.description: Verbatim passage from the source that evidences the relationship.
context.example: Ada joined Acme Corp in 2024 as a staff engineer.

relationship_id.description: Unique identifier for this relationship instance.

timestamp.description: RFC3339 timestamp associated with this item.

chunk_id.description: Chunk that provides evidence for this relation.

relations.description: Evidence entries for this relationship triplet.

Revenue grew 23% quarter over quarter.

deprecated.description: Whether this response concerns a deprecated field or route.

preferred_field.description: The canonical replacement for the deprecated field.

detail.description: Structured error detail with code, message, and deprecation hints.

backfill_chunk_interval_seconds.description: Internal interval for async backfill paging.

lookback_days.description: How far back the first sync fetches historical data. Only applies to the initial sync — subsequent syncs are incremental from the last cursor.

resources.description: Resources to activate for this connector. Each item corresponds to one entry from the Discover endpoint.

auth_type.description: Authentication method for the provider connection (e.g. `api_token`, `oauth`).

collection.description: Default collection partition for synced objects. Deprecated alias: `sub_tenant_id`.

database.description:
Database/Collection are the canonical v2 names; TenantID/SubTenantID are
their deprecated aliases, reconciled by the TenantAliases middleware before
binding so TenantID is always populated. Neither is marked binding:required
(mirroring TenantCreateRequest): a caller may send either spelling, and the
tenant scope is validated downstream by resolveTenant. Requiring tenant_id
here would force the generated SDK to demand the deprecated field.

deployment_id.description: Internal deployment context for this connector.

plan.description: Subscription plan this connector runs under.

provider_account_scope.description: Identifier for the external account (e.g. Slack workspace ID, GitHub org name). Must be distinct across connectors for the same provider.

sync_engine.description: SyncEngine selects the sync pipeline: "classic" (default) or "moveit".

sync_interval_seconds.description: How frequently the scheduler triggers incremental syncs, in seconds.

additional_metadata.description: Free-form key-value pairs to merge into the source's `additional_metadata`.

collection.description: Collection scope. Defaults to the default collection when omitted. Formerly `sub_tenant_id`; the `sub_tenant_id` alias is still accepted (deprecated).

database.description:
Database/Collection are the canonical v2 names; TenantID/SubTenantID are
their deprecated aliases. The TenantAliases middleware reconciles them in
the request body before binding, so the handler reads TenantID/SubTenantID.

database_metadata.description: Schema-backed metadata fields to merge into the source's `metadata` (database metadata). Canonical name; `tenant_metadata` is a deprecated alias.

deprecated.description: Whether this response concerns a deprecated field or route.

deprecated_since.description: API version when the field was deprecated.

message.example: tenant_id is deprecated; use database instead.

preferred_field.description: The canonical replacement for the deprecated field.

auth_type.description: Authentication method for the provider connection (e.g. `api_token`, `oauth`).

added_fields.description: Names of the metadata schema fields successfully added.

database.description: Owning database. Formerly `tenant_id`; the `tenant_id` alias is still accepted (deprecated).

additional_metadata.description: Key-value pairs merged into document metadata on every synced object from this resource.

metadata.description: Key-value pairs merged into tenant metadata on every synced object from this resource.

provider_metadata.description: Additional provider-supplied metadata for this resource.

resource_id.description: Resource identifier from the Discover endpoint.

resource_type.description: Type of resource within the provider (e.g. `channel`, `repo`, `linear_team`).

tenant_id_override.description:
DatabaseOverride/CollectionOverride are the canonical v2 names;
TenantIDOverride/SubTenantIDOverride are their deprecated aliases.

additional_metadata.description:
AdditionalMetadata is merged into the additional_metadata layer of every
object synced from this resource. Provider-generated fields take precedence.

collection.description:
Collection is the canonical v2 name for the per-resource sub-tenant
override: routes synced objects from this resource into a specific
collection. Empty means the resource inherits the connector collection.
SubTenantID is the deprecated alias for this field, reconciled by
Configure before toResource runs.

database.description:
Database is the canonical v2 name for the per-resource tenant override:
routes synced objects from this resource into a specific database.
Empty means the resource inherits the connector database. TenantID is the
deprecated alias for this field, reconciled by Configure before
toResource runs.

metadata.description:
Metadata is merged into the tenant metadata layer of every object synced
from this resource. System fields (connector_id, provider) take precedence.

resource_id.description: Resource identifier from the Discover endpoint.

resource_type.description: Type of resource within the provider (e.g. `channel`, `repo`, `linear_team`).

sub_tenant_id.description:
Routes synced objects from this resource into a specific sub-tenant
partition. Overrides the connector-level sub_tenant_id. Deprecated: use
collection.

tenant_id.description:
Optional per-resource tenant override (for "route specific resources to
different tenants"). Empty means the resource inherits the connector
tenant. Deprecated: use database.

collection.description: Collection scope. Defaults to the default collection when omitted. Formerly `sub_tenant_id`; the `sub_tenant_id` alias is still accepted (deprecated).

database.description: Owning database. Formerly `tenant_id`; the `tenant_id` alias is still accepted (deprecated).

deprecation.description:
Deprecation lists any migration nudges that apply to this request — the
caller used a legacy /tenants route, a legacy tenant_id/sub_tenant_id field,
or the deprecated sub_tenant_ids selector. It is a non-breaking signal (the
status code is unchanged); omitempty keeps it absent for fully-migrated
requests. A list so independent deprecations coexist without clobbering.

request_id.description: Unique identifier for this request, useful for support and tracing.

error_code.description: Machine-readable code for the indexing failure, empty string on success.

error_message.description: Human-readable description of the indexing failure, empty string on success.

failed_count.description: Number of uploaded files that failed to queue.

success_count.description: Number of files successfully queued for processing.

error.description: Error message for this file, empty string on success.

relations_created.description: Number of graph relations extracted from this file.

relations_error.description: Error message from relation extraction, if any.

source_fields.description:
SourceFields filters by well-known source fields such as title, type,
description, url, and timestamp.

sources.description: Retrieved knowledge sources or memories for this page.

total.description: Total number of items across all pages.

collection.description: Collection scope. Defaults to the default collection when omitted. Formerly `sub_tenant_id`; the `sub_tenant_id` alias is still accepted (deprecated).

database.description:
Database/Collection are the canonical v2 names; TenantID/SubTenantID are
their deprecated aliases (reconciled here in UnmarshalJSON and centrally by
the TenantAliases middleware).

ids.description: When provided, only items with these IDs are returned. Pagination and filters still apply.

include_fields.description: Field projection — only the listed fields plus id, database, collection are returned. Only applies to type=knowledge.

search.GraphContext.description:
GraphContext is omitted entirely when graph_context is disabled on the
request (pointer + omitempty), so the response carries no graph slice
instead of an empty-but-present object.

chunk_id_to_group_ids.description: Mapping from chunk ID to the relation group IDs it participates in.

chunk_relations.description: Scored relation paths relevant to the query, grouped by chunk.

example.combined_context: Acme Corp deploys HydraDB in production for context retrieval.

query_paths.description: Scored relation paths ranked by relevance to the query.

example.combined_context: Acme Corp deploys HydraDB in production for context retrieval.

synthesis_context.description: LLM-synthesized summary of the most relevant graph entities and relationships.
synthesis_context.example: Related entities: Acme Corp, HydraDB, production deployment.

search.MetadataFilters.description: Filters results by source metadata. Top-level keys target tenant metadata (for example department, priority, active, or tags). Nested additional_metadata keys target document metadata. Values are exact-match scalars or arrays for set-equality/list-style filters.

relation.description: Relation properties including predicate and confidence score.

additional_context.description: Optional context string prepended to the query to improve retrieval relevance.
additional_context.example: The user is a senior engineer onboarding to the platform.

alpha.description: Weighting balance between dense and sparse retrieval in hybrid mode. `"auto"` lets HydraDB choose; a number from 0 (full BM25) to 1 (full dense) sets it explicitly.

collection.description: Collection scope. Defaults to the default collection when omitted. Formerly `sub_tenant_id`; the `sub_tenant_id` alias is still accepted (deprecated).

collections.description: Preferred /query scope selector. Send either a list of collection IDs for equal normalized weighting, or an object mapping collection ID to a positive relative ranking weight with at most one decimal place. Do not send together with the deprecated sub_tenant_ids or sub_tenant_id.

database.description:
Database is the canonical v2 name for the tenant scope. TenantID is its
deprecated alias and remains fully accepted. The TenantAliases middleware
reconciles the two before binding, so TenantID is always populated and the
handler reads it; Database/Collection are carried only for docs/OpenAPI.

graph_context.description: Whether to include graph context in the response. Defaults to true for /query when omitted.

graph_vector_prune.description:
GraphVectorPrune switches the graph-connected-chunks lane from "fetch
graph-selected chunks and let the fusion reranker sort them out" to "fetch
a wider graph-selected candidate pool, then rank that pool by Milvus vector
similarity, fully replacing the final chunk list." Works in either fast or
thinking mode. Default false preserves existing behavior. Also gated
server-side by a repo-level config flag (SearchService's
graphVectorPruneEnabled) — if that flag is off, this is forced to false
regardless of what the request sets, so a deployment can disable the
mechanism without any client-side change.

graph_vector_prune_spacy_entities.description:
GraphVectorPruneSpacyEntities: when GraphVectorPrune is also set, swaps the
graph lane's entity-extraction source from the default LLM-based extractor
to a local spaCy subprocess (faster, no network round trip, but a
narrower/mismatched entity vocabulary versus the graph's own LLM-extracted
node names). No-op if GraphVectorPrune is false (including when forced
false by the server-level flag) or no spaCy extractor was configured at
startup.

ids.description:
IDs optionally scopes retrieval to specific source ids. The v2 wire field is
`ids` (matching /context/list); empty means search the whole corpus. Applied
as a Milvus `source_id in [...]` pre-filter that is preserved across the
metadata zero-result retry, so a source-scoped search that matches nothing
returns nothing rather than silently widening to the whole corpus.

max_results.description: Maximum number of chunks to return.

num_related_chunks.description: Number of adjacent chunks to pull alongside each matched chunk for additional context.

query.example: Which mode does the user prefer?

query_apps.description: Whether to include app-aware knowledge retrieval. Applies to knowledge hybrid queries.

query_by.description: Retrieval method to use for the query.

query_forceful_relations.description: Whether to force relation expansion for graph-aware query retrieval. Defaults to true when omitted.

recency_bias.description: Recency boost applied to ranking. 0 disables it; higher values favour more recent sources.

sub_tenant_id.description: Deprecated for /query (since 2.0.1). Use collection for a single scope or collections for multiple. Backwards-compatible and will be removed in a future version. Do not send together with a multi-scope selector.

sub_tenant_ids.description: Deprecated for /query (since 2.0.1). Use collections instead; it accepts the same list or weighted-object shape. Backwards-compatible and will be removed in a future version. Do not send together with collections.

type.description: Corpus to query: knowledge, memory, or all.

combined_context.description: Merged text from all chunk passages in this relation path.
combined_context.example: Acme Corp deploys HydraDB in production for context retrieval.

group_id.description: Unique identifier for this relation group.

relevancy_score.description: Relevance score for this item against the query.

source_chunk_ids.description: IDs of the chunks that contribute to this relation path.

triplets.description: Knowledge-graph triplets that make up this relation path.

app_external_id.description: Provider-assigned identifier for this source (e.g. Slack channel ID).

app_kind.description:
App-source fields (populated when the source comes from an app integration).
Default null on the wire when absent.

description.example: Internal overview of the Project Phoenix rollout.

metadata.description:
Pydantic aliases (see VectorStoreChunk). Source metadata defaults to {} on
the wire (Python default_factory=dict), unlike chunk metadata which is null.

timestamp.description: RFC3339 timestamp associated with this item.

title.description: Title or name of the source.

url.description: URL to the original source, if available.

search.SourceType.description:
Source is the wire field `type` (Python QueryRequest.source has alias="type").
SourceLegacy accepts the pre-rename `source` key (Python populate_by_name=True
keeps the field name valid on input); resolveSourceAlias folds it into Source.

additional_metadata.description:
Pydantic aliases (see VectorStoreChunk): document_metadata→additional_metadata,
tenant_metadata→metadata. FastAPI serializes by_alias, so the wire uses the aliases.

chunk_content.example: HydraDB supports hybrid retrieval across knowledge and memories.

chunk_uuid.description: Unique identifier for this individual chunk.

extra_context_ids.description: IDs of adjacent chunks pulled in as surrounding context.

metadata.description: Schema-backed tenant metadata attached to the source.

relevancy_score.description: Relevance score for this item against the query.

source_last_updated_time.description: RFC3339 timestamp when the source was last modified.

source_title.description: Title of the parent source document.

source_upload_time.description: RFC3339 timestamp when the source was ingested.

additional_context.description: Map of chunk ID to chunk content for sources declared as related by the author (query_forceful_relations).
additional_context.example: The user is a senior engineer onboarding to the platform.

chunks.description: Retrieved and ranked chunks from the knowledge store or memories.

example.chunk_content: HydraDB supports hybrid retrieval across knowledge and memories.

database_metadata_schema.description: Defines database-level metadata fields for exact-match filtering and semantic/BM25 search. Canonical name; `tenant_metadata_schema` is a deprecated alias. Schema field names are immutable after database creation.

info.description: HydraDB Application API — knowledge ingestion, search, and memory management.
info.title: HydraDB Application API

get.description: List every provider in the supported_connectors control-plane table (availability, sync engine, maturity, category) for the dashboard connector catalog.

post.description: List a provider's resources directly from supplied credentials, before creating a connector.

get.description: List all connectors for the authenticated org, optionally filtered by provider.

post.description: Create a connector for a provider and store its credentials.

get.description: Without ?id: returns every supported connector with its availability, maturity, category, and sync engine (the connector catalog). With ?id=<provider>: returns what that provider stores and how to use it — indexed_object_types (the streams that become searchable documents), searchable_fields (rendered into the indexed text), filterable_fields (each with the exact filter_key to pass in a query's metadata_filters), and the credential_schema for connecting it.

parameters.description: Provider name (e.g. slack, gmail). Omit to list all.
parameters.name: id

get.summary: List supported providers, or describe one in detail

delete.description: Delete a connector, its resources, and stored credentials.

get.description: Fetch a single connector by ID.

post.description: Save the selected resources for a connector and trigger initial sync/backfill.

patch.description: Internal-service endpoint (X-Cortex-Secret) used by the MOVEIT refresh loop to persist a rotated refresh_token for OAuth-bundle connectors. Providers that rotate the refresh token on each exchange invalidate the previously stored one, so the new token must be written back or the next sync fails with invalid_grant. Only refresh_token is merged onto the current stored credential bundle and re-encrypted under the connector's identity context; credentials are never returned.
patch.summary: Rotate a connector's stored OAuth refresh token

get.description: List a connected provider's resources using the connector's stored credentials.
List the configured resources for a connector.

post.description: Add a resource mapping to a connector.

delete.description: Remove a resource mapping from a connector.

post.description: Start a manual sync workflow for a connector.

delete.description: Delete one or more knowledge sources or memories by ID.

post.description: Ingest knowledge documents or memories for a tenant.

requestBody.description: Content type: 'knowledge' or 'memory' | Database (canonical name for the tenant scope) | Collection (canonical name for the sub-tenant scope) | Deprecated alias for database | Deprecated alias for collection | Upsert existing content (true/false/1/0) | Knowledge files to ingest (repeatable; type=knowledge) | Per-document metadata as a JSON array (type=knowledge) | App-knowledge items as a JSON array (type=knowledge) | Memory items as a JSON array (type=memory) | Optional bring-your-own-graph payload as JSON

get.description: Fetch a previously ingested source's content, inferred content, and a downloadable URL.

parameters.name: database

parameters.description: Collection (canonical name for the sub-tenant scope)
parameters.name: collection

post.description: List knowledge sources or memories (id + metadata) for a tenant.

parameters.description: Database (canonical name for the tenant scope)
parameters.name: database

parameters.description: Collection (canonical name for the sub-tenant scope)
parameters.name: collection

get.description: Return the processing status for one or more source IDs.

parameters.description: Database (canonical name for the tenant scope)
parameters.name: database

parameters.name: collection

patch.description: Merge/upsert database_metadata and additional_metadata for one source. collection is required.

delete.description: Delete a database and all associated data

get.description: List all databases for the authenticated user

post.description: Create a new database with optional custom metadata schema

get.description: List all collections for a given database
Get collection statistics for a database
Check the infrastructure provisioning status for a database

patch.description: Add new metadata schema fields to an existing database. Additive only — existing fields cannot be deleted or retyped.

post.description: Unified query endpoint that dispatches across type (knowledge/memory/all) and query_by (hybrid/text). Prefer sub_tenant_ids for sub-tenant scoping; legacy sub_tenant_id is deprecated for /query and cannot be sent together with sub_tenant_ids.

delete.description: Unregister the indexing webhook for this API key.
delete.summary: Delete the registered webhook for this API key

get.description: Get the registered indexing webhook configuration for this API key.
get.summary: Get the registered webhook for this API key

post.description: Register the indexing webhook for this API key's org.

get.description: List recent webhook delivery attempts with optional cursor-based pagination.
Get a single webhook delivery attempt by ID.
get.summary: Get a single webhook delivery attempt

post.description: Queue a failed or permanently-failed delivery for retry.
post.summary: Retry a failed or permanently failed delivery

post.description: Send a test webhook event to the registered endpoint to verify connectivity.
